_meta.deliverable: New TDD-first plan to fix the remaining randomized course-select minimap screenshot defects
_meta.planning_mode: TDD-first, screenshot-guided structural fix, no per-track exceptions
_meta.primary_goal: Eliminate the remaining screenshot-visible course-select minimap defects by locking the failing bend/side-wall/body-shape families in tests, then fixing raster/emitter behavior structurally.

observed_issue_families.name: convex_corner_wedge
observed_issue_families.description: Convex bends keep an extra diagonal shoulder/wedge or taper incorrectly at the upper-right style of corner instead of resolving to a clean stock-like bend.

observed_issue_families.name: straight_wall_shelf_or_notch
observed_issue_families.description: Long side walls show a rectangular shelf, pocket, or side-thickness mismatch along a straight wall instead of a smooth stock-like side run.

observed_issue_families.name: lower_side_detached_residue
observed_issue_families.description: Lower-left or lower-side residue remains outside the main loop as a tiny detached or weakly attached artifact family that is still screenshot-visible.

observed_issue_families.name: road_body_shape_drift
observed_issue_families.description: Some remaining defects involve the gray/white road body or shoulder shape, not only black contour topology, so full-layer raster assertions are required.

evidence_sample.current_read: The previous roadmap fixed many black-contour topology failures, but these sampled screenshots show residual raster/body-shape defects at bends and side walls that the existing tests did not lock.

current_failures: Convex bends can still render an extra diagonal wedge or incorrect taper in the course-select preview.
Straight side walls can still render a rectangular shelf, notch, or thickness mismatch.
Lower-side detached or weakly attached residue is still screenshot-visible in some tracks.
The remaining failures are not only black-contour-component problems; they also involve road-body/shoulder shape drift.
The recurrence across multiple tracks indicates a structural raster/emitter issue rather than a slug-specific exception case.
root_cause_hypothesis: `renderStyledPixelsFromRoadMask()` currently derives the stock-style 2px right-side thickness from global +X mask shifts (`rightWideMask`) rather than local tangent-aware wall semantics, which can create shelves or wedges at bends and side walls.

`emitLegalContourPreview()` and the remaining cleanup passes primarily reason about outline-only fragments, so road-bearing or road-adjacent body-shape defects can survive if they stay attached to the main structure.

Existing tests strongly cover black-topology invariants, seam survival, marker independence, and roadless-fragment pruning, but they do not yet lock screenshot-visible bend/body cleanliness at the full styled-pixel level.
things_that_must_not_happen_again: No per-track or per-screenshot production branches.
No broad cleanup heuristic that silently shaves valid seams or re-breaks marker independence.
No new fix that only improves black topology while leaving visible road-body shelves or wedges in place.
No claim of success based only on a generated ROM or a single screenshot; the failure family must be encoded in tests first.

locked_decisions.image_review_policy: Inspect fail*.png only in capped batches of <= 5 images per iteration to avoid tool instability.
locked_decisions.primary_scope: Fix preview/asset generation, especially raster and emitter semantics, before considering any runtime-only workaround.
locked_decisions.marker_policy: Start marker handling remains a separate concern and must stay independent from contour/body fixes.
locked_decisions.validation_scope: Confidence comes from synthetic fixtures, fixed-seed regressions, deterministic fuzzing, workspace ROM builds, canonical verify, and targeted screenshot-family spot checks.
locked_decisions.new_test_requirement: The next tranche must add full-layer styled-preview assertions, not only black-mask topology checks.

global_acceptance: The sampled convex-corner wedge family is reproduced in tests and then eliminated without harming legal seam bridges or right-side thickness rules.
The sampled straight-wall shelf/notch family is reproduced in tests and then eliminated without collapsing valid side-wall thickness.
The sampled lower-side detached-residue family is reproduced in tests and then eliminated in both asset-preview and rendered-output paths.
New assertions inspect full styled pixels (`pixels` plus `road_pixels` semantics), not only black connected-component counts.
All previously green seam/marker/orphan/topology tests remain green.
Deterministic seed-fuzz and workspace ROM validation remain green.
Canonical `verify.bat` remains bit-perfect after source changes.

phases.title: Capture The Remaining Screenshot Failure Model
phases.goal: Translate the sampled fail*.png evidence into explicit structural/raster classes before changing production code again.
exit_criteria: Every sampled highlighted defect maps to at least one structural/raster class.
The next tests target full styled-preview layers, not just black topology.

tasks.title: Classify the sampled fail*.png artifacts into structural raster families
acceptance: The sampled screenshots are classified into convex-corner wedge, straight-wall shelf/notch, lower-side residue, and road-body shape drift families.
The classification is phrased in raster/emitter terms rather than track-specific naming hacks.
tasks.notes: Sampled only five images due tool stability limits: fail1.png, fail1-highlighted.png, fail2-highlighted.png, fail3-highlighted.png, fail4-highlighted.png. The visible defects are not limited to black contour topology; several are road-body/shoulder shape artifacts at bends and straight walls.

tasks.title: Inspect additional fail*.png images only in capped batches if a new family appears after the first fixes
acceptance: Any later image review stays capped at five images per iteration.
New image sampling is only used if the first screenshot-derived fixes leave an unexplained family behind.

phases.title: Lock The Screenshot Families In Tests
phases.goal: Create failing tests that express the sampled screenshot defects as durable raster and rendered-output contracts.
exit_criteria: Each sampled screenshot family has at least one focused failing test or synthetic fixture.
The tests distinguish road-body shape drift from pure black-topology failures.

tasks.title: Add screenshot-derived synthetic fixtures for corner wedges, side shelves, and lower-side residue
acceptance: Fixtures exist for: convex upper-right wedge, straight right-wall shelf, lower-left detached residue, and side-wall notch/shelf variants.
At least one fixture intentionally fails against the current raster/emitter behavior before the fix lands.
tasks.notes: Added synthetic road-mask fixtures in `tools/tests/minimap_synthetic_fixtures.js` for a convex-corner shelf/wedge, a straight-wall shelf/notch family, and a lower-side residue family. These now drive the new raster contract suite.

tasks.title: Create a dedicated minimap raster contract suite for full-layer assertions
acceptance: The new suite checks visible raster cleanliness at bends and side walls using styled `pixels` plus `road_pixels` semantics.
The suite proves that black-topology-green output can still be raster-wrong, preventing another false closeout.
tasks.notes: Added `tools/tests/test_minimap_raster_contract.js` with full-layer failing assertions for the sampled screenshot families. The first run fails on five cases: synthetic convex-corner shelf, synthetic straight-wall shelf, synthetic lower-side residue, plus fixed-seed San Marino and Hungary windows from seed 42.

tasks.title: Add fixed-seed regression tests for the screenshot tracks from the current test ROM
acceptance: Seed `SMGP-1-01-42` regressions explicitly cover `san_marino`, `brazil`, `hungary`, and `west_germany`.
The assertions target the screenshot-visible families: corner wedge, wall shelf/notch, and lower-side residue.
tasks.notes: The first screenshot-driven fixed-seed tranche now includes failing raster-layer windows in `tools/tests/test_minimap_raster_contract.js` and a failing generated-asset invariant in `tools/tests/test_generated_minimap_assets.js` showing that `san_marino`, `brazil`, `hungary`, and `west_germany` currently leave all course-select overlay-reserved local tile slots blank. This gives a concrete TDD handle for the final-screen missing-border defect rather than only an intermediate preview render mismatch.

tasks.title: Preserve existing seam, marker, orphan, and right-side-thickness contracts while adding the new tests
acceptance: All previously fixed seam/marker/orphan/right-thickness tests remain present and green.
The new screenshot-family tests extend the contract instead of replacing the earlier one.

phases.title: Fix Raster And Emitter Semantics
phases.goal: Repair the actual source of the sampled screenshot defects, starting at raster semantics and only using bounded cleanup when tests prove it is necessary.
exit_criteria: The sampled screenshot families are eliminated by structural code changes, not by track-specific hacks.
Any remaining cleanup pass is clearly bounded to a tested defect class.

tasks.title: Audit `renderStyledPixelsFromRoadMask()` and `styleRoadPreview()` for tangent-incorrect wall thickening
acceptance: The code path that creates the sampled wedges/shelves is identified with a failing test first.
The audit explicitly evaluates whether global +X right-side expansion (`rightWideMask`) is causing bend and straight-wall artifacts.
tasks.notes: Audit now covers both the raster path and the actual course-select packed-screen path. Confirmed that the screenshots cannot be explained by the raw generated preview path alone: the final course-select screen still consumes `Track_preview_tilemap_data`, and generated assets had been leaving overlay-referenced local tile slots blank. Replaced the old blank-reserved-slot invariant with a final-screen compatibility invariant in `tools/tests/test_generated_minimap_assets.js`, then fixed a shared emitter regression by adding generic split-tail contour pruning for road-adjacent lower-tail outline runs that formed an illegal third black contour component. The asset suite is back to green (`50 passed`). Additional screenshot-driven TDD in `tools/tests/test_minimap_raster_contract.js` now targets the real final composed preview for `hungary`, `west_germany`, and `italy`. A key structural discovery is that all screenshot tracks use the geometry-centerline fast path in `buildGeneratedMinimapPreview()`, which currently skips the candidate-pipeline rescue variants (`expandLowerTail`, `collapseShortestSegment`, `smoothClosedPoints`) that may be needed to prevent lower-tail border loss and black-component fragmentation. Remaining open failures are isolated to that shared preview-generation/styling tranche plus the synthetic shelf/residue fixtures.

tasks.title: Fix convex-corner wedge emission without breaking stock-style right-side thickness
acceptance: Convex bend fixtures and fixed-seed screenshot-track regressions pass.
Straight right-wall 2px thickness tests remain green.

tasks.title: Fix straight-wall shelf/notch artifacts at side walls and cell boundaries
acceptance: Side-wall shelf/notch fixtures and fixed-seed regressions pass.
The fix does not reintroduce detached seam cleanup damage or false contour components.

tasks.title: Remove remaining lower-side residue families in both asset-preview and rendered output
acceptance: The sampled lower-left/lower-side residue family is eliminated in both asset-preview and final rendered minimap output.
Any cleanup used for this remains structural, bounded, and test-proven.

tasks.title: If needed, add a bounded road-bearing cleanup pass for screenshot-visible body-shape defects
acceptance: Only add this if raster fixes alone cannot solve the screenshot families.
Any new cleanup targets a named tested class such as convex-corner wedge or side-wall shelf and must not rely on slug-specific logic.

phases.title: Prove The New Fix On Real Randomization
phases.goal: Demonstrate that the new screenshot-driven fixes survive real seeds, packing, and ROM generation.
exit_criteria: The screenshot-guided regressions are green on the known failing seed.
Fuzz and workspace ROM validation remain green.

tasks.title: Run fixed-seed screenshot-family regression on the current test seed
acceptance: The new regression tests pass for `san_marino`, `brazil`, `hungary`, and `west_germany` under seed 42.
The tracks no longer show the sampled highlighted families.

tasks.title: Re-run deterministic fuzz and workspace ROM validation after the raster/emitter changes
acceptance: Seed fuzz stays green.
Workspace ROM build and minimap patch validation stay green.

tasks.title: Close the new screenshot-fix tranche with canonical root verify
acceptance: All minimap suites are green.
The new raster contract suite is green.
Workspace ROM rebuild passes.
Canonical `verify.bat` passes.

phases.title: Document The Expanded Contract
phases.goal: Record that screenshot-visible raster/body-shape failures require full-layer tests in addition to contour-topology tests.
exit_criteria: The repo explains why the previous closeout missed these families.
Future contributors know to add raster-layer tests for visible course-select defects.

tasks.title: Document the new screenshot-derived raster contract once the fixes land
acceptance: The docs explain that full-layer styled-preview cleanliness is a separate contract from black-topology cleanliness.
The screenshot-derived bend/shelf/residue families are mapped to the new tests.
